refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site-specific duty cycle context, making the requirement verifiable and traceable to operational constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct field trials under representative duty cycle conditions; collect cycle time data via onboard telemetry; compute p95 percentile; compare against CT_target; validate under safety-compliant operational modes.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with minimal idle/transition time.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote command execution with latency ≤ acceptable_threshold to support cycle time targets.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall permit cycle time optimization without compromising safety-critical constraints.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry data for performance monitoring.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing and sequencing to minimize load→travel→dump transition delays.
DP-B: Implement predictive teleoperation command queuing with latency compensation to reduce operator-induced cycle delays.
DP-C: Design safety services to operate in parallel with cycle execution, using non-blocking safety checks and pre-validated motion envelopes.
DP-D: Establish real-time telemetry pipeline to capture cycle phase timestamps and compute p95 metrics continuously.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A + DP-C + DP-D (integrated approach): Optimize vehicle actuation sequencing (DP-A) while ensuring safety services operate non-blocking (DP-C) and establish continuous telemetry-based cycle time monitoring (DP-D). DP-B is deferred to teleoperation sub-level decomposition.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing (DP-A) must respect safety envelope constraints (DP-C); requires interface definition between Vehicle and Safety Services.
Telemetry collection (DP-D) depends on vehicle sensor availability and network bandwidth; requires interface definition between Vehicle and Telemetry pipeline.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). No circular dependencies detected. Safety-first constraint is honored by design (DP-C ensures safety gates are never bypassed).

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle hydraulic/mechanical system is capable of achieving CT_target with optimized sequencing; no fundamental hardware redesign required.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services can operate with <5% cycle time overhead via non-blocking checks.
RISK: If vehicle actuation is already near physical limits, DP-A may yield insufficient improvement; mitigation: early feasibility study on vehicle dynamics.
RISK: Telemetry latency or data loss could mask true cycle time performance; mitigation: redundant timestamp logging at vehicle and ground station.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this level. DP selection is sound and traceable to FR. Proceed to IM assignment and sub-level decomposition.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Owns vehicle actuation sequencing optimization (DP-A), sensor data collection for cycle timing, and coordination with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface. Responsible for delivering cycle time performance within safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives safety envelope constraints and motion inhibit signals from Safety Services; used to gate actuation commands.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits cycle phase timestamps, actuation state, and performance metrics to telemetry pipeline for p95 computation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives load/travel/dump task commands from operator or autonomous planner; triggers cycle execution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle is the natural owner of actuation sequencing (DP-A) and cycle timing instrumentation. Minimal diffusion: only SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces cross boundary.
Safety Services owns safety constraint logic (DP-C) but does not own vehicle actuation or telemetry collection. High diffusion if cycle timing logic is split across Vehicle and Safety Services.
Teleoperation is responsible for command latency (DP-B, deferred), not vehicle actuation or telemetry. Assigning DP-A or DP-D to Teleoperation would create high diffusion.

NGHE Solution is the system-level container but does not directly implement vehicle actuation or telemetry. Assigning DP-A/D to NGHE Solution would bypass Vehicle module and create architectural diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module contains actuation sequencing logic (DP-A), cycle timing instrumentation (DP-D), and SafetyAlert/Telemetry interface handlers. Safety Services provides constraint inputs via SafetyAlert; Telemetry pipeline consumes Vehicle telemetry output. This assignment minimizes diffusion and aligns with physical system architecture.
containment_risks: RISK: If vehicle firmware is legacy/immutable, DP-A optimization may require hardware redesign or external controller; mitigation: early assessment of vehicle control system flexibility.
RISK: Telemetry collection (DP-D) may require new sensor hardware or CAN/network integration; mitigation: confirm sensor availability and network bandwidth in Vehicle module scope.
RISK: Safety Services may impose overly conservative constraints that prevent DP-A from achieving CT_target; mitigation: collaborative design of safety envelope with Safety Manager stakeholder.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm with Vehicle module owner (likely Equipment OEM or Vehicle Systems team) that actuation sequencing optimization and telemetry instrumentation are within scope. If vehicle firmware is locked, escalate to system architect for alternative DP (e.g., external motion controller).

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (DP-A + DP-C + DP-D) directly addresses FR-2 (cycle time KPI). DP-A optimizes vehicle actuation to reduce cycle time; DP-C ensures safety is not compromised; DP-D enables measurement and verification. No gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry collection (DP-D) spans Vehicle (sensor/timestamp generation) and external Telemetry pipeline (aggregation/p95 computation); interface is well-defined (Telemetry output interface) and acceptable.
Safety constraint enforcement (DP-C) spans Vehicle (actuation gating) and Safety Services (constraint generation); interface is well-defined (SafetyAlert input interface) and acceptable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is the primary owner of cycle time performance (DP-A, DP-D). Safety Services and Telemetry pipeline are external consumers/providers. Containment score 0.95 for Vehicle is high; diffusion is minimal and managed via explicit interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via Telemetry interface (DP-D). Field trials can collect p95 data under duty cycle conditions. Vehicle module can be tested independently for actuation timing and in integration with Safety Services for constraint compliance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-2 is allocated to Vehicle module with clear sub-allocations: DP-A (actuation sequencing) → Vehicle firmware/control logic; DP-C (safety compliance) → Vehicle + Safety Services interface; DP-D (telemetry) → Vehicle sensors + Telemetry pipeline. Ownership and interfaces are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Confirm Vehicle module scope includes actuation sequencing optimization and telemetry instrumentation; escalate if vehicle firmware is immutable.
Define SafetyAlert interface contract (constraint format, update frequency, latency) in collaboration with Safety Services module owner.
Define Telemetry interface contract (timestamp precision, cycle phase encoding, data rate) to ensure p95 computation accuracy.

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) that require further refinement and DP selection. Vehicle module is the primary owner, but sub-level decomposition is needed to detail actuation sequencing logic (FR-2.1), teleoperation latency (FR-2.2), safety constraint integration (FR-2.3), and telemetry pipeline (FR-2.4). Proceeding to next level will clarify module boundaries and interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with minimal idle/transition time.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote command execution with latency ≤ acceptable_threshold to support cycle time targets.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall permit cycle time optimization without compromising safety-critical constraints.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry data for performance monitoring.

assumptions: Vehicle hydraulic/mechanical system is capable of achieving CT_target with optimized sequencing.
Safety Services can operate with <5% cycle time overhead via non-blocking checks.
Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network, pipeline) is available and can support real-time cycle timing data.
Site conditions and duty cycle are well-defined and stable for p95 measurement.
open_risks: Vehicle actuation may already be near physical limits; DP-A may yield insufficient improvement.
Vehicle firmware may be immutable, requiring external controller or hardware redesign.
Safety Services constraints may be overly conservative, preventing cycle time optimization.
Telemetry latency or data loss could mask true cycle time performance.
evidence_needed: Vehicle dynamics study: confirm actuation sequencing optimization potential.
Vehicle control system assessment: confirm firmware flexibility or identify alternative control architecture.
Safety Services interface specification: define SafetyAlert constraint format and latency.
Telemetry infrastructure assessment: confirm sensor availability, network bandwidth, and p95 computation accuracy.
Field trial plan: define duty cycle, measurement methodology, and acceptance criteria for p95 ≤ CT_target.
